# Tiger Studio Manager

> Desktop companion for the TigerTag RFID filament-tracking ecosystem. Electron app — Vanilla JS + Firebase + NFC.

Tiger Studio Manager is an Electron desktop application for managing 3D-printing filament spools tagged with TigerTag RFID chips. It provides real-time Firestore inventory sync, live 3D printer integration for 6 brands, NFC chip read/write, hardware sensors (color sensor, scale), rack storage management, shareable wishlists and a friends system — all in a single window without a build step (no bundler).

**Positioning:** this app is one piece of the wider **TigerSystem** ecosystem, and deliberately a laboratory rather
than the destination — a demonstration of what open, documented technology allows once a spool can identify itself.
It is neutral and agnostic by design: TigerTag takes no side between filament brands, printer makers or
distributors. The protocol is open and anyone can build a different ecosystem on it; nothing in this repo is a
prerequisite for doing so.

Current version: **2.14.1** — macOS (Intel + Apple Silicon, signed & notarized), Windows, Linux.

## Architecture

- **Entry point**: `main.js` (Electron main process)
- **Renderer**: `renderer/inventory.html` + `renderer/inventory.js` (a single ES module, ~28 500 lines, already importing ~29 sub-modules) + modular CSS in `renderer/css/`
- **Preload bridge**: `preload.js` — exposes IPC handlers to renderer via `window.electronAPI`
- **NFC**: `services/nfc-process.js` runs as a `utilityProcess` (not a Worker Thread) to avoid SIGABRT on macOS; communicates via IPC
- **Printer integrations**: `renderer/printers/<brand>/index.js` — one file per brand, loaded dynamically
- **IoT sensors**: `renderer/IoT/td1s/` (TD1S color sensor), `renderer/IoT/tigerscale/` (TigerScale ESP32)
- **CSS**: `renderer/css/00-base.css` → `70-detail-misc.css` (10 files, loaded in numeric order via `<link>` tags)
- **i18n**: `renderer/locales/` — 9 JSON files (en/fr/de/es/it/zh/pt/pt-pt/pl), managed via `npm run i18n:add`
- **TigerTag SDK**: `@tigertag/sdk` npm package — parse, verify, encode TigerTag NTAG chips (pages 4–39, 144 bytes, blockSize=4)

## Key source files

- `renderer/CODEMAP.md` — maps every feature in `inventory.js` to a line range and anchor function name. **Read this before grepping inventory.js.**
- `renderer/inventory.js` — all application logic: state management, Firestore subscriptions, UI rendering, NFC handling, printer comms, friends system, telemetry
- `main.js` — IPC handlers: `app:info`, `rfid:encode-cloud`, `rfid:write-chip`, `rfid:read-chip`, `rfid:reader-update`, window management, auto-update
- `preload.js` — contextBridge: exposes `electronAPI` (IPC wrappers), `td1s` (TD1S sensor events)
- `services/nfc-process.js` — NFC utilityProcess: reader lifecycle, NTAG read (READ_START_PAGE=4, READ_LENGTH=144, READ_BLOCK_SIZE=4), card-present events

## State object (renderer)

```
state = {
  inventory,        // raw Firestore docs { [spoolId]: data }
  rows,             // normalizeRow() output array
  selected,         // open detail panel spoolId
  nfcReaders,       // Set<readerName> — connected readers
  nfcCardPresent,   // Map<readerName, {uid}> — readers with a card on them
  friends, friendRequests,
  publicKey,        // XXX-XXX discovery code
  privateKey,       // 40-char hex access token
  isPublic, isAdmin, debugEnabled,
  lang, i18n, viewMode, sortCol, sortDir,
  products,         // { [keyHash]: product identity } — buy links, price, attachments, favourite
  lists,            // { [listId]: user-made list of product identities }
  containerOverrides, // { [containerId]: { containerWeight } } — per-account calibration
  activeAccountId, displayName, db
}
```

## Firestore data model

```
publicKeys/{key}           → { uid, claimedAt }
userProfiles/{uid}         → { publicKey, displayName, isPublic, color_* }
publicLists/{token}        → world-readable snapshot of a list shared by web link (unguessable
                             token); written by the owner when a list's visibility is "public"
users/{uid}                → { displayName, email, publicKey, privateKey, isPublic,
                               roles, tier, Debug,   ← roles + tier are SERVER-ONLY (rules reject client writes)
                               studioVersion, studioElectron, studioPlatform, studioArch,
                               studioOsRelease, studioOsVersion, studioLang, studioLocale,
                               studioLastSeen }
  /inventory/{spoolId}     → { uid, id_brand, id_material, color_name, online_color_list,
                               weight_available, container_weight, container_id, capacity,
                               updatedAt, deleted, twin_uid }
  /telemetry/studio        → { sessionsCount, versionsUsed[], platformsUsed[], lastSeen,
                               td1sUsed, rfidReadersMax }
  /friends/{friendUid}     → { displayName, addedAt, key }
  /friendRequests/{uid}    → { displayName, requestedAt, key }
  /blacklist/{uid}         → { displayName, blockedAt }
  /products/{keyHash}      → product identity shared by every spool of it: label, custom image,
                               buyUrl + price (HT/TTC), min stock, note, tags, favourite,
                               attachments[] (web links, tier-capped 1/2/5)
  /lists/{listId}          → user-made lists of product identities (itemKeys[]), shareable
  /racks/{rackId}          → rack layout docs
  /printers/{brand}/devices/{id}
                           → one doc per known printer of that brand (connection + identity)
  /printerSeen/{brand:id}  → { ts } last-seen stamp per printer, drives the online/offline column
  /prefs/app               → { lang }
  /scales/{mac}            → TigerScale heartbeat
  /apiKeys/{docId}         → API key docs
  /telemetry/{docId}       → aggregated usage metrics
  /uidMigrationMap/{id}    → decimal → hex UID migration
  /containerOverrides/{id} → per-account container calibration { containerWeight } — overrides the
                               bundled catalogue weight at resolution time, never mutates it
  /rfidList/{chipUid}      → physical chips seen by this account (firstSeen; a `backup` hex dump
                               means TigerTag+)
  /notifications/{id}      → friend-to-friend notifications (field-whitelisted in the rules)
  /apps/studio             → per-app state flags (e.g. the onboarding version already run)
```

Chipless spools have `spoolId` starting with `TigerData_` (minted for new entries) or the legacy `CLOUD_` (older docs, not migrated — both recognised by `_isChiplessId`). `normalizeRow()` sets `r.isCloud = true` for either. On encoding, the Cloud doc is promoted: `spoolId` replaced by the chip UID, Cloud doc hard-deleted.

## Spool tiers (user-facing vocabulary)

Three tiers, visible as badges throughout the UI:

| Tier | What it is | Data |
|---|---|---|
| **TigerData** | fully digital, no physical chip | `spoolId` = `TigerData_<id>` (or legacy `CLOUD_<id>`); `r.isCloud === true` |
| **TigerTag** | a written NFC chip | `spoolId` = chip UID |
| **TigerTag+** | a certified chip with a signed factory dump | UID + a `backup` hex dump in `rfidList` |

**TigerData was called "TigerCloud" until v2.13.0.** As of v2.14.0 the rename reaches stored values too:
`normalizeRow` computes `protocol: "TigerData"` for chipless (so `syncSpoolMirrors` writes it to every doc), and
chipless doc ids migrate `CLOUD_` → `TigerData_` silently on login (`maybeMigrateCloudToTigerData`; both prefixes
still recognised forever by `_isChiplessId`, no server sweep). **One value stays `TigerCloud`: the `byProtocol`
telemetry rollup key** (`functions/index.js` buckets chipless under `"TigerCloud"`, derived from the id prefix, not
the field) — kept stable so historical analytics don't fragment. So: doc field + display + filter = `TigerData`;
only the analytics bucket key is still `TigerCloud`.

## Inventory interchange — `.ttag` files (v2.14.0)

A `.ttag` file is a portable, offline snapshot of one or more inventory materials — the unit for backup, USB
transport and sharing. Shape: `{ format:"tigertag", kind:"ttag", version:1, exportedAt, exportedBy, records:[…
verbatim inventory docs …], rfidBackups? }`. Firestore Timestamps are normalised to epoch-ms; twins are atomic
(one material → both twin records); a legacy `CLOUD_` id is refused on export (only `TigerData_` chipless may
leave). `exportedBy` (owner uid) is a UX hint for import mode, never a security boundary. Import parses →
validates the marker (`version ≤ 1`) → **sanitises** every record (untrusted input: non-http(s) URL fields dropped,
colour/weight/TD clamped) → previews → writes in two modes: **Restore** (verbatim, keeps ids / TigerTag+ / the
`rfidList` backup) or **Import** (fresh chipless `TigerData_` spools you own — new `id_tigertag` nonce,
`id_product` unset, backups dropped, weight reset to capacity, `twin_tag_uid` remapped). Code:
`_buildTtag` / `_exportMaterialTtag` (export), `openTtagImportPicker` / `_ttagValidate` / `_ttagSanitizeRecord` /
`_ttagApplyImportAll` (import), all in `renderer/inventory.js`.

## NFC / RFID system

- **ACR122U** via `nfc-pcsc` in a `utilityProcess` — avoids SIGABRT on macOS
- **NTAG read**: `reader.read(4, 144, 4)` — start page 4, 144 bytes (36 pages), blockSize=4 (critical: prevents sliding-window overlap)
- **Chip encode**: `TigerTag.fromCloudDoc(doc).patch({ timestamp }).toBytes(false)` → 80 bytes → write pages 4–0x17
- **Multi-reader**: up to 2 simultaneous readers; `state.nfcCardPresent` Map tracks which reader has a card
- **TigerPOD**: open-source dual reader/writer stand — 3D-printable shell + two USB readers (repo https://github.com/TigerTag-Project/TigerPOD, free STL MakerWorld #1289152)

RFID badge in header: always visible. Disconnected = filled red gradient pill, clicking opens TigerPOD modal. Connected = green tint. Card-present = filled green gradient.

TigerPOD modal opens from four triggers: (1) cloud spool banner click with no reader, (2) "Please update RFID" banner click with no reader, (3) red RFID badge click, (4) `+ Scan` button click with no reader.

## Printer integrations

Each brand has `renderer/printers/<brand>/PROTOCOL.md` (self-contained spec) and `index.js` (implementation).

| Brand | Protocol | Port |
|---|---|---|
| Bambu Lab | MQTTS TLS | 8883 |
| Creality | WebSocket | 9999 |
| Elegoo | MQTT | 1883 |
| FlashForge | HTTP poll + TCP M-codes | 8898 / 8899 |
| Snapmaker | Moonraker WebSocket | 7125 |
| Anycubic | MQTTS TLS 1.2 (LAN) + signed REST / cloud MQTT | 9883 |

All brands: printer discovery (mDNS + port scan + manual IP), filament edit per slot, camera widget.

## Sensors

- **TD1S** (`renderer/IoT/td1s/`) — USB color + TD sensor. `window.td1s.onStatus(cb)` supports multiple listeners (IPC `on`, not `once`). Scan color (1–3 slots) and TD value independently or together.
- **TigerScale** (`renderer/IoT/tigerscale/`) — ESP32 scale over WebSocket. Live weight, container/filament split, TARE (hold-to-confirm), twin UID grid.
- **USB scale (Dymo M-series)** (`renderer/IoT/usbscale/`) — HID scale opened in the main process (`node-hid`, vid `0x0922`, usage page `0x8D`), streamed over IPC (`usb-scale-update` / `usb-scale-data`). One-write-per-pose state machine: POD mode saves silently when reader chip UIDs identify the spool; side-card mode offers an inline confirm. Live UI in the open spool's WEIGHT section (`#usbScaleDock`).

## i18n system

`t(key, params?)` — looks up `state.i18n[state.lang][key]`, fallback chain: requested lang → en → key itself. Supports `{{param}}` interpolation, `["array"]` random pick, `{"one":"…","other":"…"}` plurals.

**Never edit locale JSON files by hand.** Use:
```bash
npm run i18n:add -- myKey --after existingKey en="..." fr="..." de="..." es="..." it="..." zh="..." pt="..." pt-pt="..." pl="..."
npm run i18n:check   # validates consistency (also runs as pre-commit hook)
```
1403 keys × 9 locales as of v2.14.0.

## CSS conventions

- One dimension + `aspect-ratio` for SVG mask icons (never both `width` and `height`)
- Asset URLs: `url('../../assets/svg/icons/…')` from `renderer/css/` (two `..` to project root)
- Specificity: double class selector instead of `!important` when fighting global rules
- Hold-to-confirm buttons: `setupHoldToConfirm(el, durationMs, cb)` with `<span class="hold-progress"></span>` child

## Key JS patterns

- **Modals**: `.modal-overlay` + `.modal-card`, toggled via `.open` class
- **Resizable panels**: `makePanelResizable(panelEl, handleEl, storageKey)` — drag handle, localStorage persistence
- **Weight slider auto-save**: 500 ms debounce → Firestore write; `.wb-saving` pulse class during window
- **Health indicator**: `#health` cloud icon; `fromCache === false` → green, `true` → red; lazy RTT ping on `mouseenter`
- **Telemetry**: two-level — `users/{uid}` (last-known, overwrite) + `users/{uid}/telemetry/studio` (lifetime, FieldValue.increment/arrayUnion). Guards `_telTd1s` / `_telRfidMax` prevent duplicate writes per session.

## Auth & security

- Firebase Auth (Google OAuth loopback PKCE + email/password)
- `displayName` always from Firestore `users/{uid}.displayName` (pseudo), never from Firebase Auth `user.displayName` (Google real name)
- `roles`, `tier` and `Debug` fields writable only via Firebase Admin SDK / Cloud Function — never by client
  (`tier` gates the attachment cap: free 1 / plus 2 / premium 5, enforced server-side)
- Firestore Security Rules enforce owner-only access; friends granted read on `inventory`, `racks`, `products` and
  `lists` via presence in the owner's `friends/` subcollection. **The rules live in a separate repo**
  (`TigerTag_Firebase_Backend/firestore.rules`), not here
- Several collections are **field-whitelisted** (`hasOnly([...])`) — an unlisted field is rejected *silently* on the
  client, so adding a field means updating the rules and redeploying

## LocalStorage keys

`tigertag.accounts` · `tigertag.activeAccount` · `tigertag.inv.<id>` · `tigertag.view` · `tigertag.lang` · `tigertag.sidebar` · `tigertag.panelWidth.detail` · `tigertag.panelWidth.debug`

## Build & release

```bash
npm start                          # dev (no bundler)
npm run i18n:check                 # validate all 9 locales
git tag v2.13.0 && git push --tags  # triggers CI: parallel build macOS/Windows/Linux → GitHub Release
```

CI: `.github/workflows/build.yml` — Apple Developer ID signing + notarytool on macOS, electron-builder on all platforms, publishes to GitHub Releases.

## Ecosystem repositories

Tiger Studio Manager is the desktop app of a wider open hardware + software ecosystem.

| Project | What it is | Repository |
|---|---|---|
| TigerTag SDK for JavaScript | Parse, verify and encode TigerTag NFC chips — used internally by this app | https://github.com/TigerTag-Project/TigerTag-SDK-JS |
| TigerTag SDK for Python | Same operations in Python, for scripts and automation | https://github.com/TigerTag-Project/TigerTag-SDK-Python |
| TigerScale | ESP32 firmware + hardware for the open-source filament scale | https://github.com/TigerTag-Project/Tiger-Scale |
| TigerPOD | Open-source dual NFC reader/writer stand for spools — 3D-printable shell + two USB readers | https://github.com/TigerTag-Project/TigerPOD |

## Optional docs

- `renderer/CODEMAP.md` — line ranges for every feature in `inventory.js`
- `ROADMAP.md` — what's done / in progress / backlog, grouped by domain
- `DEVLOG.md` — per-session engineering journal
- `renderer/printers/<brand>/PROTOCOL.md` — self-contained agent skill per printer brand
- `TIGERTAG_FIREBASE_INTEGRATION.md` — third-party Firebase integration guide
- `FEATURES.md` — consolidated catalogue of every shipped feature, tagged by version
- `CODEMAP-main.md` — line ranges for every section of `main.js`
